pages.title: Observability for shipping teams
pages.description: Dense, fast, AI-aware observability workflows built for engineers who ship every day.

sections.title: Observability that keeps up with you.
sections.body: Logs, traces, metrics, and LLM generations in one dense surface. Shaped around the keyboard-first workflows engineers actually use — not screenshots of competitor dashboards.

sections.title: The real UI, with a frozen snapshot.
sections.body: Every widget below ships in the product — same StatCards, same uPlot charts, same flamegraph. Toggle a tab to explore; numbers are static so the page stays fast.

items.title: Logs that scale
items.body: Virtualised explorer with live tail, server-side facets, and boolean filters. Click a value, never leave the page.

items.title: Fast traces
items.body: Flamegraphs, service maps, and span detail without waiting on re-renders. Critical-path highlighting built in.

items.title: Metric queries
items.body: Aggregations, formulas, and time-series tuned with uPlot. Pan a month of data without drops.

items.title: AI workflows
items.body: First-class LLM generations, sessions, prompts, datasets, and evals — not bolted on after the fact.

items.title: Actionable alerts
items.body: Rule builder, silences, and routing that stay readable at a thousand rules and still dispatch in seconds.

items.title: OTLP native
items.body: Send from anything that speaks OpenTelemetry. No agents. No vendor SDKs. No lock-in.

sections.title: Built to disappear
sections.body: A frontend that gets out of the way. Keyboard-first, dense by default, one shortcut palette for every route and entity.
highlights: Command palette covers every route, entity, and recent page.
Every explorer shares facets, analytics, and visualisations.
Time range, team, and theme persist across sessions.
URL-shareable state — paste a link, land on the exact view.

sections.title: Ready when you are.
sections.body: Sign in to explore. No credit card for the evaluation tier.

pages.title: A tighter surface for logs, traces, metrics, and AI
pages.description: Keep the Datadog-grade density. Remove the sprawl. Make the happy path obvious.

sections.title: Dense. Fast. Navigable.
sections.body: Every page is shaped by a real investigation — not by a design review. Shareable URLs, stable facets, and zero modal mazes.

items.title: Structured log search
items.body: Server-side facets, regex, booleans. Click a value to pivot — no second click, no modal.

items.title: Flamegraph + span table
items.body: Correlate a slow span with its logs in one click. Critical-path spans are highlighted automatically.

items.title: Formula-driven charts
items.body: Aggregate, ratio, threshold — inline. No nested editors. No JSON spelunking.

items.title: Generation explorer
items.body: Dataset runs, eval scores, and prompt revisions side-by-side. Diff two runs in seconds.

items.title: Silences that make sense
items.body: Scope by service, instance, or arbitrary label. Expire automatically. Audit every mute.

items.title: URL-shareable state
items.body: Paste a link, land on the exact view — filters, time range, drawer selection, and all.

sections.title: Keyboard-first, always
sections.body: The command palette is the primary surface. Every route, every entity, every recent page reachable in one shortcut.
highlights: Every table row is selectable without a mouse.
Facet rails respond to ↑/↓ and Enter.
Time range picker has keyboard shortcuts for every preset.

pages.title: Predictable pricing for engineering teams
pages.description: Usage-aware pricing for infrastructure telemetry and LLM workloads. No per-seat games.

sections.title: Pay for what you ingest. Nothing else.
sections.body: No per-seat games. No mystery add-ons. One price for telemetry volume, one for LLM generations. Everything else — unlimited.

items.title: Telemetry GB
items.body: Logs, traces, and metrics billed by compressed ingest. Same rate across all three types — no category games.

items.title: LLM generations
items.body: Per-generation billing for AI workloads. Dataset evaluations and eval scores are included.

items.title: Unlimited users
items.body: Invite the whole team. Roles, audit logs, and SSO in every tier from day one.

items.a: Yes — for evaluation workloads, with the same feature set as paid tiers. Ingest up to 5 GB and 5k generations per month before moving to metered billing.
No. Every tier includes unlimited users, roles, and audit logs.

items.q: What counts as an LLM generation?
items.a: A single completion call. Streaming partials are rolled up into one generation, so streamed responses don't cost more.
Yes — see the Self-host page. Commercial license available for teams with egress, compliance, or data-residency constraints.
Compressed bytes over the wire, calculated at the collector. Same number you'll see in your OTel export logs.

pages.title: Built around compatibility, density, and speed
pages.description: A frontend shaped around real observability workflows instead of generic dashboards.

sections.title: Density and speed aren't afterthoughts.
sections.body: Every layer — query defaults, virtualisation, chart primitives, WebSocket buffering — is budgeted for Datadog-scale workloads from the first commit. Enforced in CI.

items.title: uPlot everywhere
items.body: No heavy chart runtime. 60 fps panning at 10k points per series, with flicker-free refreshes via setData().

items.title: Virtualised explorers
items.body: Live-tail and paginated views render only visible rows. Page sizes of 500+ without DOM pollution.

items.title: Query-standard defaults
items.body: One wrapper for staleTime, retry, and keepPreviousData. No flashing skeletons on refresh.

items.title: Feature isolation
items.body: ESLint-enforced. No cross-feature imports. Shared code lives in @shared. Regressions caught in PR.

items.title: Module-owned dashboards
items.body: Every domain registers its own panels. Adding a panel is one TSX file, not a plumbing exercise.

items.title: CI architecture budgets
items.body: Bundle budgets per feature chunk. UI runtime hard-capped at 200 KB. Fails fast, never drifts.

sections.title: Budgeted from day one
sections.body: The frontend holds to an explicit architecture budget, measured in every PR.
highlights: Chunk size budgets on every feature.
No TS enums. No as any. No cross-feature imports.
Strict loading-state convention: isPending && data === undefined.
Zustand for UI state, TanStack Query for server state — never mixed.
Lockfile-pinned vendor versions; dependency churn tracked in CI.

pages.title: OpenTelemetry in, fast insight out
pages.description: Adopt OTLP without losing the navigability and workflow depth engineers actually need.

sections.title: OTLP is the only contract.
sections.body: Point any OpenTelemetry exporter at the collector and the platform picks up traces, logs, and metrics. No agents. No vendor SDKs. No proprietary protocols.

sections.title: Send your first span
sections.body:
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com
export OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=checkout
export OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES=deployment.environment=production

# That's it. Any OTel SDK (Node, Go, Python, Java, Rust, Ruby,
# .NET, PHP) will start flowing to Optikk.

items.title: Full OTLP spec
items.body: Spans, logs, metrics — HTTP and gRPC, batched and streaming. No fields dropped.

items.title: Semantic conventions
items.body: Service maps and instrumentation inference rely on the OTel spec directly — your standards-based instrumentation just works.

items.title: No SDK lock-in
items.body: Switch vendors tomorrow. Your instrumentation is standards-based; the platform is the replaceable piece.

pages.title: Self-host when control matters
pages.description: Run the platform close to your telemetry while keeping the same dense UI model.

sections.title: Bring the platform to your telemetry.
sections.body: Run Optikk inside your VPC with the same UX as the managed tier. Same binaries, same charts, same query engine, same release cadence.

items.title: Single binary
items.body: One Go binary per region. Horizontal scale on commodity hardware. Helm chart + Terraform included.

items.title: Your keys, your rules
items.body: Bring your own KMS, OIDC, and audit sink. Zero telemetry data leaves your network perimeter.

items.title: Same UI
items.body: No feature splits between cloud and self-host — the frontend is the exact same artifact.

sections.title: Get the self-host pack.
sections.body: Helm chart, Terraform module, runbook, and a commercial license. We'll walk you through the first deploy.
